frameworks.name: optimal foraging theory
frameworks.one_line: maximize rewards minus efforts per unit time as fitness objective

frameworks.name: effort discounting
frameworks.one_line: effort cost of harvest scales decisions and movement vigor jointly

frameworks.name: movement vigor
frameworks.one_line: saccade and lick speed treated as economic readout of value

frameworks.name: neuromodulatory state control
frameworks.one_line: pupil-linked brainstem signal coordinates decision and motor circuits

core_question: Why do economic variables such as reward and effort jointly modulate decisions and movement vigor during foraging?
key_assumptions: fitness is captured by reward minus effort divided by time (capture rate)
decisions and movements share a common economic currency
pupil size indexes brainstem neuromodulatory state
manipulations: effort cost of harvest, varied by displacing the food tube relative to the mouth
key_findings: higher harvest effort lengthened work duration (more stockpiling before harvest)
higher harvest effort reduced saccade and lick vigor
pupil constricted under high harvest effort and dilated as effort declined
decisions, movements, and pupil state co-varied as a global brain-state shift
interpretations: brainstem neuromodulatory circuits controlling pupil size alter both decision and motor computations to encourage work and conserve energy
this coordinated response improves capture rate, a fitness-relevant variable
